Package.Name: Scripting
Package.Summary: Run complete JavaScript with Pocok.Scripting and inspect returned values, limits, and structured failures.

Fields.ExpectResultHelp: When enabled, null and undefined are returned as a structured missing-result failure.

Result.Title: Execution result
Result.Empty: Run the current script to inspect its value or structured failure.

Tips.Title: What to notice
Tips.Isolation: Each run uses a fresh Jint engine, so scripts cannot share mutable engine state.
Tips.Bounds: Timeout, statements, recursion, source length, memory, queueing, and output each have separate limits.
Tips.ReturnValue: The final JavaScript expression becomes the returned value. A declaration alone usually evaluates to undefined.
Tips.Failures: JavaScript errors and limit violations are returned as structured failures instead of host exceptions.
Tips.Capabilities: Hosts expose approved values and delegates explicitly; no ambient capabilities are available.

Purpose.Title: Purpose
Purpose.Body: Pocok.Scripting provides a host-controlled JavaScript boundary for automation and extension points without exposing ambient CLR access.

QuickStart.Title: Quick start
QuickStart.Body: Write a complete script and leave the value to inspect as the final expression. The example invokes ScriptRunner directly.

Results.Title: Results
Results.Body: Successful runs return the final JavaScript value. The Showcase formats scalar, array, and object results deterministically and does not expose captured exception objects.

Bounds.Title: Execution bounds
Bounds.Body: ScriptRunner limits timeout, statements, recursion, source length, memory, and cancellation. The Showcase also bounds queueing, input, and output.

Capabilities.Title: Explicit capabilities
Capabilities.Body: Hosts may register approved scalar values and delegates through ScriptBinding. Complex CLR objects and ambient interop remain unavailable.

Imports.Title: Imports and modules
Imports.Body: The package can resolve comment-based imports from an explicit module source. This public deployment runs self-contained scripts and does not provide a persistent module catalog.

Concurrency.Title: Concurrency
Concurrency.Body: ScriptRunner creates a fresh engine for every request and supports concurrent callers. Each Showcase run has its own scope, cancellation source, result channel, and progress channel.

Production.Title: Production notes
Production.Body: Treat scripts as untrusted input, keep limits conservative, authorize every delegate, exclude secrets from diagnostics, and remember that in-process limits are not operating-system isolation.

Snippet.ScriptTitle: Complete script with a return value

Assist.Object: Return an object literal as the final expression.
Assist.Reduce: Reduce an array to one value.

Boundaries.Title: Boundaries
Boundaries.NoClr: No ambient CLR or reflection access.
Boundaries.NoIo: No filesystem, network, browser, or service-provider access.
Boundaries.FreshEngine: A fresh engine and script state for every run.
Boundaries.Bounded: Separate limits for source length, timeout, statements, recursion, memory, queueing, and output.

Source.Title: Source documentation
Source.Body: Package documentation for execution limits, capabilities, imports, and security boundaries.

arithmetic.Name: Function return value
arithmetic.Description: Define a function and return the triangular number of 12 from the final expression.

object-result.Name: Object and array result
object-result.Description: Return an object containing aggregate and transformed array values.

string-result.Name: Template string
string-result.Description: Return a JavaScript template string from a complete script.

missing-result.Name: Missing result
missing-result.Description: End with a declaration while a result is required and inspect the structured failure.

syntax-error.Name: Syntax failure
syntax-error.Description: Run invalid JavaScript and inspect its safe diagnostic location.

recursion-limit.Name: Recursion limit
recursion-limit.Description: Trigger the configured recursion boundary without affecting the host process.
